REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES FILED BY DEFENDANT PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY UNUM GROUP (FKA UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY AND UNUM GROUP'S (FKA UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS OBJECTED TO THE SUMMARY OF SURGICAL LOGS AND THE FINANCIAL ANALYSIS THAT ARE EXHIBITS TO THE ARNOLD - GARRY DECLARATION. THE OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED. THE DECLARATION DOES NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT FACTS AS TO THE SOURCE OF THE DATA AND/OR THE PREPARATION OF THE EXHIBITS. DEFENDANT ALSO HAS NOT PROVIDED THE FOUNDATION TO ADMIT THE EXHIBITS UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 622, 623, 1220 OR 1221. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF, THE DECLARATIONS OF DR. MACGREGOR AND VANESSA HILL CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS PERMANENTLY DISABLED. (COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE MACGREGOR DECLARATION.) BECAUSE DEFENDANT RELIES ON THE SAME FACTS IN SEEKING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, THAT MOTION IS DENIED FOR THE SAME REASONS. THE FULLER DECLARATION ALSO CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE HANDLING OF THE CLAIM. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5797
JURY FEES, TRAN # W2208610J018, TAG # 021072 TRUST RECEIPT NO. 21072 DEPOSITED BY PLAINTIFF MAHJOURI, FARHAD S M.D., M.P.H., F.I.C.S.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY AND UNUM GROUP'S (FKA UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: LAWRENCE MANN, ESQUIRE AND BENNETT M. COHEN, ESQUIRE (BOURHIS & MANN, 415.392.4660) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS. JOHN T. BURNITE, ESQUIRE (WILSON ELSEN, 415.433.0990) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS OBJECTED TO THE SUMMARY OF SURGICAL LOGS AND THE FINANCIAL ANALYSIS THAT ARE EXHIBITS TO THE ARNOLD - GARRY DECLARATION. THE OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED. THE DECLARATION DOES NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT FACTS AS TO THE SOURCE OF THE DATA AND/OR THE PREPARATION OF THE EXHIBITS. DEFENDANT ALSO HAS NOT PROVIDED THE FOUNDATION TO ADMIT THE EXHIBITS UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 622, 623, 1220 OR 1221. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF, THE DECLARATIONS OF DR. MACGREGOR AND VANESSA HILL CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS PERMANENTLY DISABLED. (COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE MACGREGOR DECLARATION.) BECAUSE DEFENDANT RELIES ON THE SAME FACTS IN SEEKING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, THAT MOTION IS DENIED FOR THE SAME REASONS. THE FULLER DECLARATION ALSO CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE HANDLING OF THE CLAIM. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Severing This Action
Off Calendar Feb-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY AND UNUM GROUP'S (F.K.A. UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION) MOTION FOR AN ORDER SEVERING THIS ACTION.
APPEARANCES: BENNETT COHEN, ESQUIRE (BOURHIS & MANN, 415.392.4660) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHN T. BURNITE, JR. (KELLY HERLIHY & KLEIN, 415.951.0535) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT. ANNE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL (OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL, DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE, 415.703.1403) APPEARING FOR THE DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT DOES NOT SEE THAT SEVERANCE WOULD ACHIEVE ANYTHING FOR THE FRESNO ACTION AND THIS ACTION COULD BE COORDINATED WITHOUT DOING SO. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO AMEND ITS PLEADING TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER, THIS REQUEST WILL BE MOOT. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Feb-20-2008 Continued To Apr-11-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT:
APPEARANCES: LAWRENCE MANN, ESQUIRE AND BENNETT COHEN, ESQUIRE (BOURHIS & MANN, 415.392.4660) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ANNE M. BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL (OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL ON BEHALF OF THE DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE, 415.703.1403) APPEARING FOR THE DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE. JOHN T. BURNITE, JR. (KELLY HERLIHY & KLEIN, 415.951.0535) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT PROVIDENT LIFE & ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the California Settlement Agreement and the Decision and Order of Insurance Commissioner Upon Settlement. The record reflects that the Commissioner has reviewed and withdrawn the subject policy and as a consequence, the relief plaintiff seeks already has been obtained. There is no authority to order the retroactive withdrawal of a policy. Peterson v. American Life & Health Ins. Co. (1995) 48 F.3d 404, 411. With respect to the duty of the Commissioner to oversee claims practices, such acts involve discretionary conduct (Ins. Code sections 790.05 and 790.035) and the Commissioner does not adjudicate claims between insurers and insureds. (Ins. Code section 12921.4(a)). PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anne Michelle Burr of the Office of the Attorney General, 703-1403, appearing for defendant and moving party

No other appearances. This matter was not argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS. THE WRIT MUST BE VERIFIED. THE AMENDMENT MUST SPELL OUT THE MANDATORY DUTIES THE COMMISSIONER ALLEGEDLY HAS FAILED TO PERFORM AND MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER INSURANCE CODE SECTION 790.03 AND 10291.5. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: PATRICK HEARD, CSR #11055
